web_server.notes: Server is functional for core agent+session flows but is missing ~14 endpoints that the frontend api-client maps. Those calls silently return undefined (the api-client falls back to undefined for unmapped 404s). Interactive approval/question resolution, model switching, permission management, MCP management, Praxis multi-agent, retry/edit-resend/regenerate/undo, context compaction, config, tools list, and session search are all missing from the Rust web server.

missing_coverage: WebSocket event streaming (agent token/complete events actually received)
Agent run end-to-end in web mode (submit → WS complete event → UI update)
Tool approval/question resolution flow in web mode
db-web-fallback coverage (SQL query patterns that fall through to the warn/no-op branch)

findings.issue: resolve_approval and resolve_question endpoints are absent from the web server. The frontend maps these to /api/agent/resolve-approval and /api/agent/resolve-question respectively (src/lib/api-client.ts), but neither route is registered. When an agent requests tool approval or asks a question in web mode, the frontend calls these commands, apiInvoke 404s silently (returns undefined), and the agent blocks forever waiting for a response that never arrives.
findings.recommendation: Add POST /api/agent/resolve-approval and POST /api/agent/resolve-question handlers. The web server already receives approval_request events over WS and holds the approval_rx receiver in WebStateInner — wire the HTTP handlers to forward responses into those channels. For question_request, replicate the same pattern using question_rx.

findings.issue: retry_last_message, edit_and_resend, regenerate_response, and undo_last_edit are all missing. The frontend calls these from useAgent.ts for common user actions (retry, edit+resend, regenerate, undo). In web mode every call silently returns undefined, so the UI shows no response and no error — the user sees a broken no-op.
findings.recommendation: Implement POST /api/agent/retry, /api/agent/edit-resend, /api/agent/regenerate, and /api/agent/undo in api.rs, backed by corresponding AgentStack methods (which already exist in the Tauri desktop path in src-tauri/src/commands/agent_commands.rs).

findings.issue: Model management endpoints missing: GET /api/models/current, POST /api/models/switch. The frontend uses get_current_model on startup to populate the model selector, and switch_model whenever the user changes the model. In web mode both calls return undefined — the model selector shows no current model and switches are silently dropped.
findings.recommendation: Add GET /api/models/current (delegating to AgentStack::current_model()) and POST /api/models/switch (calling AgentStack::switch_model()). Both are trivial wrappers already implemented for the Tauri path.

findings.issue: Praxis (multi-agent) endpoints are absent: POST /api/agent/praxis/start, GET /api/agent/praxis/status, POST /api/agent/praxis/cancel, POST /api/agent/leads/{id}/steer. The frontend calls rustBackend.startPraxis() from useAgent when team mode is enabled. In web mode the call 404s silently. Additionally, Praxis WS events (praxis_worker_started, praxis_worker_token, etc.) are fully converted in src-tauri/src/events.rs but the web server's WebAgentEvent enum in api.rs (line 813) does not include any Praxis variants — they are swallowed by the _ => None arm in convert_agent_event.
findings.recommendation: 1) Add the Praxis endpoints to the web server. 2) Add Praxis event variants to WebAgentEvent and handle them in convert_agent_event so the team bridge in the frontend receives them over WS.

findings.issue: clear_message_queue is a documented no-op. The handler comment says 'clearing it from the outside requires draining the mpsc channel, which isn't safe' and returns {ok:true} without clearing anything. The frontend calls this to let the user clear the steer/follow-up queue. This means in web mode the queue is unclearing even when the user explicitly requests it.
findings.recommendation: Use a separate atomic flag or an additional channel close/recreate pattern to drain the queue. Alternatively, expose the queue contents as a Vec in a RwLock in WebStateInner so the HTTP handler can drain it directly.

findings.issue: GET /api/tools and GET /api/tools/agent (list_tools, list_agent_tools) and POST /api/tools/execute (execute_tool) are missing. The frontend uses these to populate the Tools list in the Settings panel and to execute individual tools from the UI. All silently return undefined.
findings.recommendation: Add the tool endpoints backed by AgentStack's tool registry.

findings.issue: Permission management endpoints missing: GET/POST /api/permissions and POST /api/permissions/toggle. The user cannot change permission level (Standard/AutoApprove) in web mode.
findings.recommendation: Add permission endpoints. The permission level is stored in AgentStack — expose it via a simple RwLock field in WebStateInner.

findings.issue: MCP management endpoints missing: GET /api/mcp (list_mcp_servers) and POST /api/mcp/reload (reload_mcp_servers). Users cannot inspect or reload MCP servers from the web frontend.
findings.recommendation: Expose the MCP runtime from AgentStack through WebState and add the two endpoints.

findings.issue: GET /api/config (get_config) and POST /api/context/compact (compact_context) are missing. get_config is called at startup to hydrate the settings panel. compact_context is called from the /compact slash command. Both silently return undefined.
findings.recommendation: Add GET /api/config returning the serialized Config, and POST /api/context/compact delegating to the context condensation pipeline.

findings.issue: POST /api/sessions/search (search_sessions) is absent. Frontend calls this from the session search box. apiInvoke returns undefined silently, so session search yields no results in web mode.
findings.recommendation: Add the endpoint backed by session_manager.search() (or an FTS5 query if available).

findings.issue: approval_request events are forwarded over the WS channel, but resolve_approval and resolve_question endpoints do not exist on the server (see critical finding #1). This creates a deadlock: the agent sends an approval_request WS event, the frontend receives it and calls resolve_approval, that HTTP call 404s, the frontend silently swallows it (apiInvoke returns undefined for 404s), and the agent loop blocks forever waiting for a reply on the approval channel.
findings.recommendation: This is a consequence of the missing endpoints. Fix priority: implement resolve_approval and resolve_question before shipping web mode as production-ready.

findings.issue: env-bridge.ts calls `invoke('get_env_var', ...)` directly from @tauri-apps/api/core without routing through the isTauri() guard. In web mode, Tauri's invoke stub returns undefined rather than throwing. This means env-bridge silently reports all env vars as undefined in web mode, which causes provider API key detection to fail (model providers will think no API keys are configured).
findings.recommendation: Update env-bridge.ts to use invokeCommand() from rust-bridge (which already has the isTauri() guard and falls back to apiInvoke), or add /api/env/{name} to the web server.

findings.issue: vitest.config.ts includes glob patterns for packages/core-v2, packages/core, packages/platform-*, packages/extensions, cli/ and docs/examples/ in the 'include' array. Per MEMORY.md, all of those directories were deleted in 2026-03-16. The stale globs do not cause test failures (they match nothing) but they bloat the test discovery configuration and add confusion.
findings.recommendation: Remove the deleted-package globs from the include array, keeping only 'src/**/*.{test,spec}.{ts,tsx}' and 'tests/e2e/**/*.{test,spec}.ts'.

findings.issue: The Playwright webServer command is 'npx vite --port 1420', which runs the Vite dev server (Tauri mode with stub Tauri APIs), not the actual AVA web server ('ava serve'). The e2e/web-mode.spec.ts tests contain test.skip() guards that check for backend availability and skip if it's not running — but the backend is never automatically started. The web-mode tests are effectively never fully run in CI because the backend is never wired up.
findings.recommendation: Add a second webServer entry or a global setup fixture that starts 'cargo run --bin ava -- serve --port 8080' before the web-mode spec. Or separate the web-mode spec into a distinct playwright config that sets API_BASE to the running ava serve instance.

findings.issue: The web-mode.spec.ts tests do not cover WebSocket event flow at all. There are no tests that verify: (a) the WS connection is established when the app loads in non-Tauri mode, (b) a token event received over WS updates the streaming content in the UI, or (c) a complete event received over WS stops the spinner and commits the message. This is the most critical functional path in web mode.
findings.recommendation: Add a Playwright test that intercepts WS traffic using page.on('websocket', ...) and/or uses a mock server, then verifies that the UI responds correctly to token and complete WS events.

findings.issue: apiInvoke performs a naive camelCase-to-snake_case conversion (replacing uppercase letters with _lowercase). This misses nested objects — for example, SubmitGoalArgs fields like sessionId become session_id correctly, but the args.thinkingLevel inside nested objects is not converted. The backend's SubmitGoalRequest uses serde default snake_case, so mismatched field names from nested payloads will silently be None/default in Rust.
findings.recommendation: Apply the snake_case conversion recursively to nested objects, or use a typed serialization approach (e.g. serde's rename_all on the TS side via a code-generated client from the OpenAPI spec).
